title: Pilosella lobarzewskii
taxonomy: Pilosella lobarzewskii is classified within the family Asteraceae and the genus Pilosella, which belongs to the tribe Cichorieae. More specifically, it is a member of the subgenus Eunomia. This classification is part of its complex and still-evolving taxonomic history. The Pilosella genus is known for its numerous species and hybrids, making classification and identification challenging. Further research is often necessary to confirm the exact classification of Pilosella lobarzewskii.

intro: Pilosella lobarzewskii, also known as Lobarzewski's hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can provide relief from various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella lobarzewskii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pilosella lobarzewskii is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
